scheme_name: Procedure for Admission in Government Child Care Institution, Government Communication Home and Open Shelter Home
page_title: Procedure for Admission in Government Child Care Institution, Government Communication Home and Open Shelter Home

details: The scheme “Procedure for Admission in Government Child Care Institution, Government Communication Home, and Open Shelter Home” has been implemented by the Women Welfare Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to support orphaned, destitute, abandoned, neglected, and vulnerable children by providing them with clothing, food, accommodation, medical care, counselling, education, training, and other essential facilities.
benefits: In the institutions and homes, children are provided with free facilities such as clothing, food, accommodation, medical care, counselling, entertainment, training, and education. Along with these facilities, children are also provided day care facility based on their needs.
eligibility: In Government Child Care Institutions, orphaned/destitute children, abandoned children, surrendered children, exploited and neglected children, and victimized children are admitted.
In Government Communication Homes, children who are in conflict with the law or have violated the law are admitted.
In Open Shelter Homes, destitute children living on the roadside, beggars, ragpickers, and children from slums who do not attend school are admitted.

offline: Through the Child Welfare Committee and the Juvenile Justice Board, concerned children are admitted to the respective institutions. Complaints can also be lodged through the toll-free number 1098. At present, the following institutions/homes have been established in the state:
Government Child Care Institutions: A total of 05 institutions established in Dehradun, Haridwar, and Almora.
Government Communication Homes: A total of 10 homes established in Almora, Dehradun, Haridwar, Nainital, Uttarkashi, Pauri, and Udham Singh Nagar.
Open Shelter Homes: A total of 07 homes established in Dehradun, Nainital, and Haridwar.

documents_required: Application/Referral Order from Child Welfare Committee or Juvenile Justice Board
Birth certificate / Age proof of the child (if available)
Medical fitness certificate or health check-up report of the child (if available)
Police report / FIR / General Diary entry (in cases of abandoned or missing children) (if available)

faqs.question: What facilities are provided to children under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Children receive free facilities such as clothing, food, accommodation, medical treatment, counselling, education, training, and recreational activities. In addition, day care facilities are also available.

faqs.question: Who can be admitted to Government Child Care Institutions?
faqs.answer: Children who are orphaned, destitute, abandoned, surrendered, exploited, neglected, or victimized are admitted to Government Child Care Institutions.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for admission in Government Communication Homes?
faqs.answer: Children who are in conflict with the law or have violated the law are admitted to Government Communication Homes.

faqs.question: Who can be admitted to Open Shelter Homes?
faqs.answer: Street children, beggars, rag pickers, slum children, and school dropouts living in vulnerable conditions are admitted to Open Shelter Homes.

faqs.question: How are children admitted into these institutions?
faqs.answer: Admission is done through the Child Welfare Committee (CWC) and the Juvenile Justice Board (JJB) based on the child’s condition and background.

faqs.question: How many Government Child Care Institutions are there in Uttarakhand?
faqs.answer: At present, 5 Government Child Care Institutions are established in Dehradun, Haridwar, and Almora.

faqs.question: How many Government Communication Homes exist in the state?
faqs.answer: There are 10 Government Communication Homes located in Almora, Dehradun, Haridwar, Nainital, Uttarkashi, Pauri, and Udham Singh Nagar.

faqs.question: How many Open Shelter Homes are functioning in Uttarakhand?
faqs.answer: A total of 7 Open Shelter Homes are established in Dehradun, Nainital, and Haridwar.

faqs.question: How can concerned citizens help support this scheme?
faqs.answer: Citizens can help by reporting vulnerable children via helpline 1098, volunteering, spreading awareness, and supporting rehabilitation programs for children.
